Binary Analysis

Author

Simmons & Girard

Published

April 7, 2026

1. Prep Data

1.1. Libraries

library(tidyverse)
library(simhelpers)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

1.2. Directory & Loading Data

DIR <- "~/Documents/Github/MastersThesis/iccs/data"
dat_nest <- readRDS(paste0(DIR,'/binary_results_0312_01.rds'))
dat_aov_res <- readRDS(paste0(DIR,'/binary_aOV_full.rds')) #aov results

# dat_all <-  dat_nest |> left_join(dat_aov, by = c("n_raters", "n_objects","target_icc","p","seed"))

#head(dat_nest) 

#Kable (doesn't render?)
# k1 <- kable(dat_nest, format = "html") %>%
#   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover")) %>%
#   scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "200%")
# k1

1.3. Data Wrangling

## unnest the results' list-column  (by hand; example with "icc" column which is numeric) 

# dat <- dat_nest |> 
#   mutate(result = map(result, as_tibble)) |>
#   unnest(result) |>
#   mutate(
#     icc = map_dbl(icc, \(x) pluck(x, .default= NA_real_))) #example

##  unnest efficiently, let R decide the best atomic data type
dat_all <- dat_nest |> 
  mutate(result = map(result, as_tibble)) |>
  unnest(result) |>
  #remove columns for now (unnecessary)
  select(!c(approach, observed, expected, adjusted, boot_results, details, call)) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.list), \(col) {
    map_vec(col, \(x) if(is.null(x)) NA else x)
  }))

dat_aov <- dat_aov_res |>
  mutate(result = map(result, as_tibble)) |>
  unnest(result) |>
  #remove columns for now (unnecessary)
 # select(!c(approach, observed, expected, adjusted, boot_results, details, call)) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.list), \(col) {
    map_vec(col, \(x) if(is.null(x)) NA else x)
  }))

# dat <- dat_all |> 
#   left_join(dat_aov, 
#   by = c("n_raters", "n_objects","target_icc","p","seed","filename"))

#set conditions as factors
conds <- c("n_raters", "n_objects", "p")
dat_all <- dat_all |> 
  mutate(across(.cols = all_of(conds), .fns = as.factor))

dat_aov <- dat_aov |> 
  mutate(across(.cols = all_of(conds), .fns = as.factor))



# Delete nested object
rm(dat_nest)
rm(dat_aov_res)

# Print
head(dat_all)
# A tibble: 6 × 16
  n_raters n_objects target_icc p        seed filename       icc message warning
  <fct>    <fct>          <dbl> <fct>   <dbl> <chr>        <dbl> <lgl>   <lgl>  
1 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/3… 0.338 FALSE   FALSE  
2 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/3… 0.04  FALSE   FALSE  
3 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/3… 0.635 FALSE   FALSE  
4 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/3… 0.1   FALSE   FALSE  
5 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/3… 0     TRUE    FALSE  
6 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/3… 0.345 TRUE    FALSE  
# ℹ 7 more variables: error <lgl>, g_icc <dbl>, g_message <lgl>,
#   g_warning <lgl>, g_error <lgl>, kappa <dbl>, s_bp <dbl>
head(dat_aov)
# A tibble: 6 × 10
  n_raters n_objects target_icc p        seed filename       aov_icc aov_message
  <fct>    <fct>          <dbl> <fct>   <dbl> <chr>            <dbl> <lgl>      
1 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/302…   0.552 FALSE      
2 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/302…   0.503 FALSE      
3 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/302…   0.264 FALSE      
4 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/302…   0.159 FALSE      
5 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/302…   0.628 FALSE      
6 3        10               0.4 0.5   3022043 iccs/data/302…  -0.141 FALSE      
# ℹ 2 more variables: aov_warning <lgl>, aov_error <lgl>

In the binary case, the parameter grid of (54) conditions is as follows: 

design_factors <- list(
n_raters = c(3,12,24),
n_objects = c(10,50,100),
target_icc = c(0.40,0.60,0.80),
p = c(0.5, 0.8)# for the binary case
)  
#expand grid
params <- expand_grid( !!!design_factors) 
design_factors
$n_raters
[1]  3 12 24

$n_objects
[1]  10  50 100

$target_icc
[1] 0.4 0.6 0.8

$p
[1] 0.5 0.8
head(params)
# A tibble: 6 × 4
  n_raters n_objects target_icc     p
     <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl>
1        3        10        0.4   0.5
2        3        10        0.4   0.8
3        3        10        0.6   0.5
4        3        10        0.6   0.8
5        3        10        0.8   0.5
6        3        10        0.8   0.8

1.4 Wide to Long

Formatting data from wide to long allows easier use of {simhelpers} tidy function for simulations’ performance measures.

# add prefixes 
colnames(dat_all)[c(7,11)] <- paste0("pre_", colnames(dat_all)[c(7,11)])
colnames(dat_aov)[7] <- paste0("pre_", colnames(dat_aov)[7]) 

dat_all <- dat_all |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("pre_"),
    names_to = "method",
    names_prefix = ("pre_"),
    values_to =  "value",
  )

dat_aov <- dat_aov |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("pre_"),
    names_to = "method",
    names_prefix = ("pre_"),
    values_to =  "value",
  )

2. Performance Measures for Point Estimators

Here, we consider measures for point estimators for each of the 54 binary conditions

We obtain, for each condition and measure (ICC, Generalized ICC, and AOV_ICC): - Bias (defined as “Mean absolute error” by Oest & Girard, Miratrix & Pustejovsky) - Bias (defined by Muthen & Muthen) - Standard Errors (i.e., standard deviation of replication distribution) - Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) For all measures

bias_all <- dat_all |>
  #group by each condition (For ordinal, 162 total conditions)
  select(!c(seed, filename)) |>
 # group_by(n_raters, n_objects, target_icc, k_category, e_category) |> 
  summarize(
    simhelpers::calc_absolute( 
      estimates = value, true_param = target_icc, 
      criteria = c("bias","stddev","rmse")
    ), 
    .by = c(n_raters, n_objects, target_icc, p, method)
  )
    
head(bias_all)
# A tibble: 6 × 12
  n_raters n_objects target_icc p     method K_absolute    bias bias_mcse stddev
  <fct>    <fct>          <dbl> <fct> <chr>       <int>   <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl>
1 3        10               0.4 0.5   icc          1000 -0.138    0.00608  0.192
2 3        10               0.4 0.5   g_icc        1000 -0.0651   0.00779  0.246
3 3        10               0.4 0.8   icc          1000 -0.158    0.00666  0.211
4 3        10               0.4 0.8   g_icc        1000 -0.0546   0.00904  0.286
5 3        10               0.6 0.5   icc          1000 -0.186    0.00669  0.211
6 3        10               0.6 0.5   g_icc        1000 -0.0626   0.00786  0.248
# ℹ 3 more variables: stddev_mcse <dbl>, rmse <dbl>, rmse_mcse <dbl>

For AOV measures

bias_aov <- dat_aov |>
  #group by each condition (For ordinal, 162 total conditions)
  select(!c(seed, filename)) |>
 # group_by(n_raters, n_objects, target_icc, k_category, e_category) |> 
  summarize(
    simhelpers::calc_absolute( 
      estimates = value, true_param = target_icc, 
      criteria = c("bias","stddev","rmse"), 
    ), 
    .by = c(n_raters, n_objects, target_icc, p, method)
  )
    
head(bias_aov)
# A tibble: 6 × 12
  n_raters n_objects target_icc p     method  K_absolute   bias bias_mcse stddev
  <fct>    <fct>          <dbl> <fct> <chr>        <int>  <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl>
1 3        10               0.4 0.5   aov_icc       1000 -0.315   0.00984  0.311
2 3        10               0.4 0.8   aov_icc       1000 -0.333   0.00924  0.292
3 3        10               0.6 0.5   aov_icc       1000 -0.435   0.00942  0.298
4 3        10               0.6 0.8   aov_icc       1000 -0.437   0.00880  0.278
5 3        10               0.8 0.5   aov_icc       1000 -0.507   0.00816  0.258
6 3        10               0.8 0.8   aov_icc       1000 -0.529   0.00805  0.254
# ℹ 3 more variables: stddev_mcse <dbl>, rmse <dbl>, rmse_mcse <dbl>

Join together, sharing CaseNumber

bias_full <-  bias_all |> full_join(bias_aov) 
Joining with `by = join_by(n_raters, n_objects, target_icc, p, method,
K_absolute, bias, bias_mcse, stddev, stddev_mcse, rmse, rmse_mcse)`

2.1 Discussion: Point Estimates Bias

Note: all graphs have corresponding Monte Carlo Standard Error confidence intervals!

2.1.1 Bias (Mean Absolute Error)

# uniform formatting
my_geom <- list(
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)),
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)), 
  geom_errorbar( aes( ymin = bias - 1.96*bias_mcse,
                      ymax = bias + 1.96*bias_mcse ),
                 position = position_dodge( width=0.3),
                 width = 0 ), 
  geom_hline( yintercept = 0 ),
  theme_minimal() 
)

bias_long_p0.5 <- bias_full |>
  filter(p ==0.5) 

p1 <- ggplot(bias_long_p0.5, aes(n_raters, bias, 
                       col=method, pch=method, group=method ) ) +
  facet_grid( target_icc ~ n_objects, labeller = label_both ) +
  ggtitle("p=0.5") + 
  my_geom

bias_long_p0.8<- bias_full |>
 filter(p==0.8) 

p2 <- ggplot(bias_long_p0.8, aes(n_raters, bias, 
                       col=method, pch=method, group=method ) ) +
  facet_grid( target_icc ~ n_objects, labeller = label_both ) +
 ggtitle("p==0.8") + 
 my_geom

p1
p2

2.1.2 Bias (Muthen & Muthen)

  • Here, if bias is less than 10% then it is good
my_geom2 <- list(
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)),
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)), 
  geom_errorbar( aes( ymin = bias - 1.96*bias_mcse,
                      ymax = bias + 1.96*bias_mcse ),
                 position = position_dodge( width=0.3),
                 width = 0 ), 
  geom_hline( yintercept = 0 ),
  theme_minimal(), 
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.10, linetype='dotted', col = 'red'),
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_percent())
)

bias_long_p0.5 <- bias_full |>
  mutate(bias2 = bias/target_icc) |>
  filter(p==0.5) 

p3 <- ggplot(bias_long_p0.5, aes(n_raters, bias2, 
                       col=method, pch=method, group=method ) ) +
  facet_grid( target_icc ~ n_objects, labeller = label_both ) +
  ggtitle("p=0.5") + 
  my_geom2

bias_long_p0.8 <- bias_full |>
  mutate(bias2 = bias/target_icc) |>
 filter(p==0.8) 

p4 <- ggplot(bias_long_p0.8, aes(n_raters, bias2, 
                       col=method, pch=method, group=method ) ) +
  facet_grid( target_icc ~ n_objects, labeller = label_both ) +
 ggtitle("p==0.5") + 
 my_geom2


p3
p4

2.2 Discussion: Root Mean Square Error (RMSE)

# uniform formatting
my_geom <- list(
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)),
  geom_line(position = position_dodge(width = 0.3)), 
  geom_errorbar( aes( ymin = rmse - 1.96*rmse_mcse,
                      ymax = rmse + 1.96*rmse_mcse ),
                 position = position_dodge( width=0.3),
                 width = 0 ), 
  geom_hline( yintercept = 0 ),
  theme_minimal() 
)

bias_long_p0.5 <- bias_full |>
  filter(p==0.5) 

p5 <- ggplot(bias_long_p0.5, aes(n_raters, rmse, 
                       col=method, pch=method, group=method ) ) +
  facet_grid( target_icc ~ n_objects, labeller = label_both ) +
  ggtitle("p==0.5") + 
  my_geom

bias_long_p0.8 <- bias_full |>
 filter(p==0.8) 

p6 <- ggplot(bias_long_p0.8, aes(n_raters, rmse, 
                       col=method, pch=method, group=method ) ) +
  facet_grid( target_icc ~ n_objects, labeller = label_both ) +
 ggtitle("p==0.8") + 
 my_geom


p5
p6
